  "text": "The Node.js ESM loader and CJS-ESM interop bridge are implemented across multiple interconnected files.\n\n**Entry Point Detection (run_main.js):**\nThe execution starts in lib/internal/modules/run_main.js with executeUserEntryPoint() function (line 156), which determines whether to use the ESM loader or CJS loader via shouldUseESMLoader() (line 64). This function checks file extensions (.mjs, .cjs), package.json type field, and CLI flags to determine the appropriate loader.\n\n**Core ESM Loader (loader.js):**\nThe ModuleLoader class (lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js:110) is the central orchestrator for module loading. It manages:\n- Resolution caching via #resolveCache (ResolveCache from module_map.js:25)\n- Load caching via loadCache (LoadCache from module_map.js:89)\n- Multiple translator strategies via translators (SafeMap from translators.js:81)\n- CustomizedModuleLoader support for user-provided hooks via HooksProxy (hooks.js:472)\n\nKey methods include getModuleJobForImport(), which coordinates resolution and loading, and import() which is the main entry point for dynamic imports.\n\n**Module Resolution (resolve.js):**\nThe ESM specifier resolver implements the Node.js module resolution algorithm in lib/internal/modules/esm/resolve.js. The defaultResolve() function (line 1011) orchestrates package resolution through:\n- moduleResolve() (line 899) for relative and absolute specifiers\n- packageResolve() (line 801) for package name resolution\n- packageExportsResolve() (line 588) for package.json exports field resolution\nThese work together to resolve import specifiers to file URLs.\n\n**Module Translation (translators.js):**\nThe translators SafeMap (lib/internal/modules/esm/translators.js:81-82) contains format-specific translators that convert source code to ModuleWrap instances. Translators handle various formats: 'module' (ESM), 'commonjs', 'json', 'builtin', 'wasm', and CommonJS-specific variants for interop.\n\n**Module Loading (load.js):**\nThe defaultLoad() function (lib/internal/modules/esm/load.js:87) and defaultLoadSync() (line 158) retrieve module source code. They call getSource() and getSourceSync() to fetch the raw source, then validate the format through get_format.js integration.\n\n**Compilation to ModuleWrap (utils.js):**\nThe compileSourceTextModule() function (lib/internal/modules/esm/utils.js:336) compiles JavaScript source text into a ModuleWrap native object using V8's module compilation APIs.\n\n**Module Job Pipeline (module_job.js):**\nThe ModuleJob class (lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js:72) represents a single module in the module graph and implements the linking/evaluation pipeline:\n- _link() (line 114) resolves all module dependencies recursively by calling getModuleJobForImport() for each import\n- _instantiate() (line 160) instantiates all modules in the dependency graph via ModuleWrap.instantiate()\n- run() (line 262) evaluates the module via ModuleWrap.evaluate()\n\nFor require() of ESM modules, ModuleJobSync (line 310) handles synchronous loading with runSync() (line 382) performing instantiateSync() and evaluateSync().\n\n**Native ModuleWrap Binding (module_wrap.h/cc):**\nThe native ModuleWrap class (src/module_wrap.h:36) wraps V8's Module object and provides:\n- Link() (h:110) for linking module dependencies\n- Instantiate() (h:111) for instantiating the module\n- Evaluate() (h:112) for evaluating the module\n- GetModuleRequests() (h:104) for retrieving import specifiers\n- GetStatus() (h:114) for checking instantiation/evaluation state\n- CreateRequiredModuleFacade() (h:91) for CommonJS require() of ESM\n\n**CJS-ESM Interop (cjs/loader.js):**\nThe finalizeEsmResolution() function (lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:1258) provides fallback resolution when CommonJS require() attempts to load an ESM module. It validates resolved URLs and converts them to file paths. This bridges the gap between CJS require() and ESM module resolution.\n\n**Hooks System (hooks.js):**\nThe HooksProxy class (lib/internal/modules/esm/hooks.js:472) and Hooks class (line 108) enable user-provided loader hooks via module.register(). These hooks allow customization of resolve, load, and import.meta initialization without modifying core loader code.\n\nThe entire chain works as follows: executeUserEntryPoint determines ESM vs CJS -> createModuleLoader creates a ModuleLoader -> ModuleLoader.import() calls resolve() and load() -> compileSourceTextModule creates ModuleWrap -> ModuleJob manages the linking and evaluation pipeline via ModuleWrap's native methods -> ModuleWrap evaluates the ES module code and returns results to the JavaScript layer."

  "text": "The Node.js ESM loader and CJS-ESM interop bridge are implemented across multiple interconnected files.\n\n**Entry Point Detection (run_main.js):**\nThe execution starts in lib/internal/modules/run_main.js with executeUserEntryPoint() function (line 156), which determines whether to use the ESM loader or CJS loader via shouldUseESMLoader() (line 64). This function checks file extensions (.mjs, .cjs), package.json type field, and CLI flags to determine the appropriate loader.\n\n**Core ESM Loader (loader.js):**\nThe ModuleLoader class (lib/internal/modules/esm/loader.js:110) is the central orchestrator for module loading. It manages:\n- Resolution caching via #resolveCache (ResolveCache from module_map.js:25)\n- Load caching via loadCache (LoadCache from module_map.js:89)\n- Multiple translator strategies via translators (SafeMap from translators.js:81)\n- CustomizedModuleLoader support for user-provided hooks via HooksProxy (hooks.js:472)\n\nKey methods include getModuleJobForImport(), which coordinates resolution and loading, and import() which is the main entry point for dynamic imports.\n\n**Module Resolution (resolve.js):**\nThe ESM specifier resolver implements the Node.js module resolution algorithm in lib/internal/modules/esm/resolve.js. The defaultResolve() function (line 1011) orchestrates package resolution through:\n- moduleResolve() (line 899) for relative and absolute specifiers\n- packageResolve() (line 801) for package name resolution\n- packageExportsResolve() (line 588) for package.json exports field resolution\nThese work together to resolve import specifiers to file URLs.\n\n**Module Translation (translators.js):**\nThe translators SafeMap (lib/internal/modules/esm/translators.js:81-82) contains format-specific translators that convert source code to ModuleWrap instances. Translators handle various formats: 'module' (ESM), 'commonjs', 'json', 'builtin', 'wasm', and CommonJS-specific variants for interop.\n\n**Module Loading (load.js):**\nThe defaultLoad() function (lib/internal/modules/esm/load.js:87) and defaultLoadSync() (line 158) retrieve module source code. They call getSource() and getSourceSync() to fetch the raw source, then validate the format through get_format.js integration.\n\n**Compilation to ModuleWrap (utils.js):**\nThe compileSourceTextModule() function (lib/internal/modules/esm/utils.js:336) compiles JavaScript source text into a ModuleWrap native object using V8's module compilation APIs.\n\n**Module Job Pipeline (module_job.js):**\nThe ModuleJob class (lib/internal/modules/esm/module_job.js:72) represents a single module in the module graph and implements the linking/evaluation pipeline:\n- _link() (line 114) resolves all module dependencies recursively by calling getModuleJobForImport() for each import\n- _instantiate() (line 160) instantiates all modules in the dependency graph via ModuleWrap.instantiate()\n- run() (line 262) evaluates the module via ModuleWrap.evaluate()\n\nFor require() of ESM modules, ModuleJobSync (line 310) handles synchronous loading with runSync() (line 382) performing instantiateSync() and evaluateSync().\n\n**Native ModuleWrap Binding (module_wrap.h/cc):**\nThe native ModuleWrap class (src/module_wrap.h:36) wraps V8's Module object and provides:\n- Link() (h:110) for linking module dependencies\n- Instantiate() (h:111) for instantiating the module\n- Evaluate() (h:112) for evaluating the module\n- GetModuleRequests() (h:104) for retrieving import specifiers\n- GetStatus() (h:114) for checking instantiation/evaluation state\n- CreateRequiredModuleFacade() (h:91) for CommonJS require() of ESM\n\n**CJS-ESM Interop (cjs/loader.js):**\nThe finalizeEsmResolution() function (lib/internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:1258) provides fallback resolution when CommonJS require() attempts to load an ESM module. It validates resolved URLs and converts them to file paths. This bridges the gap between CJS require() and ESM module resolution.\n\n**Hooks System (hooks.js):**\nThe HooksProxy class (lib/internal/modules/esm/hooks.js:472) and Hooks class (line 108) enable user-provided loader hooks via module.register(). These hooks allow customization of resolve, load, and import.meta initialization without modifying core loader code.\n\nThe entire chain works as follows: executeUserEntryPoint determines ESM vs CJS -> createModuleLoader creates a ModuleLoader -> ModuleLoader.import() calls resolve() and load() -> compileSourceTextModule creates ModuleWrap -> ModuleJob manages the linking and evaluation pipeline via ModuleWrap's native methods -> ModuleWrap evaluates the ES module code and returns results to the JavaScript layer."
